dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 7945 IH: Nitrous Oxide Safety Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Mullin introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce

form.official-title: To ban the sale of nitrous oxide consumer products, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Nitrous Oxide Safety Act of 2026.

section.header: Nitrous oxide products considered a banned hazardous product

subsection.text: Except as provided in subsection (b), on and after the date that is 180 days after the date of the enactment of this Act, a consumer product that contains nitrous oxide shall be considered to be a banned hazardous product under section 8 of the Consumer Product Safety Act (15 U.S.C. 2057).
Nothing in this section may be construed to—

paragraph.text: ban the sale of a product that contains nitrous oxide to be used in the course of—

subparagraph.text: commercial production of food or food products, including in a manufacturing setting or commercial kitchen; or

paragraph.text: apply to a consumer product in which nitrous oxide is sold as a component of food and used as a propellant to drive such food out of a pressurized container.
The term commercial kitchen means a commercial restaurant, caterer, or other food preparation business.
The term consumer product has the meaning given that term under section 3(a)(5) of the Consumer Product Safety Act (15 U.S.C. 2052(a)(5)).
The term medical or dental treatment means care provided by a dentist, doctor, pharmacist, or another professional licensed to dispense medication.
The term nitrous oxide means the gas chemically known as N2O, which is also known as laughing gas and whippits.

The term research and development means activities conducted by an accredited academic institution, a business, nonprofit entity, or government agency engaged in scientific, engineering, medical, or technological research or the development of products or services.
